entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori UI Adaptation: Hide 'Standard' View
entry.content:
Hello, 

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud, you can achieve this requirement by using the Manage Variants functionality. Here's  how to prevent the user from selecting the Standard variant and display only the Custom variant for the Roles table:

1. Navigate to the app where the smart table object is located.

2. Click on the 'Variant Management' button on the toolbar of the smart table. This will open the Manage Variants dialog.

3. In the Manage Variants dialog, select the Custom variant that you have created for the Roles table.

4. Click on the 'Set as Default' button to set the Custom variant as the default variant for the smart table.

5. Next, click on the 'Authorization' tab in the Manage Variants dialog. Here, you can restrict the access to the Standard variant for specific roles or users. You can remove the authorization for the Standard variant for all users or specific roles.

6. Once you have set the Custom variant as the default and removed the authorization for the Standard variant, click on 'Save' to apply the changes.

this way you can prevent the user from selecting the Standard variant and display only the Custom variant for the Roles table in the Fiori app. This will ensure that the fields hidden in the Custom variant will not be accessible to the user.

BR

Harish Mangtani

entry.title: Re: SAP SCREEN PERSONAS - SAP EWM WAREHOUSE MONITOR
entry.content:
Hello

Yes, it is possible to disable a button within the Warehouse Monitor app in S/4 HANA Public Cloud based on movement type and applicable for certain users.

To do this, you would need to use the SAP Fiori UI adaptation tool to customize the Warehouse Monitor app. You can use the UI adaptation tool to configure the visibility and behavior of UI elements, such as buttons, based on certain conditions.

First, you would need to identify the specific movement types and users for which you want to disable the button. Then, using the UI adaptation tool, you can create a rule that checks the movement type and user information, and based on that rule, you can disable the button for the specified conditions.

The UI adaptation tool provides a user-friendly interface for making these customizations without the need for extensive coding. Once the rule is created and applied, the button will be disabled as per your specified conditions.

It's important to note that making these customizations may require appropriate authorizations and access to the UI adaptation tool. 

BR
Harish Mangtani

entry.title: LICKING THE GIF CYCLE
entry.content:
There are failures, and then there are failures. In hardware, the smoke test often gets you one chance. In software, you get do-overs. In art creation, you can go with the first take, or refine, or toss and start over. This failure story, is of the last kind. When I dog-fathered the #SCNotties contest here in a prior decade, the rule was more or less, be funny and be quick. Fast, as in a 30-second elevator pitch. Today, a 3 second You-Tube yell-at-you.

Roughly 15 years (or 5 SAP web app cycles) ago, I put together an animated GIF selfie using tools I had available (motto: "when you have a hammer, every task needs to be nailed"), in this case, X Windows, NetBSD, XPaint, and the aptly named gifsicle.

Here you might be saying, but Jim, (or Detective, depending on which side of the desk you're on), that is not coding. How can you toss a tiny image into a global, enterprise-scale contest, and not have impostor feebies?

Link to the post that has the image, preserved within the SAP community domain--https://community.sap.com/t5/additional-blogs-by-members/30-seconds-over-the-hill/ba-p/12874805

And what's so funny about a GIF?

What I said then was, " I created it on my NetBSD workstation using XPaint and gifsicle, and the Jimbert character Blag and I created for his comic strip, in about 90 minutes of hacking." Let's consider hacking as a synonym for coding. Many people I've met do what one may call configurating not coding. It's similar mental effort.

 

gifsicle pic1.gif pic2.gif pic3.gif > animation.gif

 --delay time

bash-4.0$ gifsicle --delay 100 cpu_micro_pool_stacked*gif >animation.gif
gifsicle: warning: so many colors that local colormaps were required
gifsicle: (You may want to try `--colors 256'.)

 

The bash version should give you a time-capsule sense of when I tried this, and the warnings hint at the limited palette for graphics icons. Delay is key to the animation working, the 100 here has no bearing on how I drew myself. Possibly on an archive somewhere I have the individual GIF files I rolled together to make the final public version, though if you want to know, the composite can be disassembled (don't ask me how I know).

 

cpu_micro_pool_stacked_220.gif: PNG image, 897 x 393, 8-bit/color RGBA, non-interlaced
cpu_micro_pool_stacked_227.gif: PNG image, 897 x 393, 8-bit/color RGBA, non-interlaced
cpu_micro_pool_stacked_307.gif: PNG image, 897 x 407, 8-bit/color RGBA, non-interlaced

 

Personal effects include the propellor head, the ponytail, the tie-dye shirt, and the 30-second stopwatch (thanks greatly to Ray Harryhausen for the novel model approach).

Selfie Cell

Notes & Comments:

  • NetBSD pkgsrc: Package gifsicle-1.93 has a denial-of-service vulnerability, see https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2023-44821
  • https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kohler/gifsicle/
  • Full tenure for the win
  • The "par" man page: "If I knew of any bugs, I wouldn't release the package. Of course,
    there may be bugs that I haven't yet discovered."
  • gifsicle (icicle, popsicle, dreamsicle, hammer and sickle ...)
  • One entry per person, this is it.

entry.title: Re: New video series for SAP Datasphere data marketplace
entry.content:
Hi Hemanta,

 

thanks for the feedback, I will definitely include a video showing the exact setup. 
As far as I understood from your question and the link you included, you are referring to the creation of an content aggregator, which is an additional concept to group and manage data providers.

Please note, that this is not a prerequisite for setting up data exchange between two tenants. To achieve the exchange you have to follow this high-level process:

1. create a Data Provider in the source tenant, with the marketplace visibility set to private data provider and/or internal data provider (depending on your use case)

2. create an internal / private context (see video part 4)

3. create the needed data products and set the context within it (see video 5)

4. go the the context management and create activation keys according to you use case (member activation keys to enable data consumption, provider activation keys to enable data sharing) 

5. in the target tenant: activate the context --> for membership go to the "join" button within "data marketplace/my contexts/", for data sharing go to "join"-button within "data sharing cockpit/context

Hope that helped, let me know in case there are some questions left open. 
As said, I will try to publish a video explaining this in detail as soon as possible.

Thanks and regards

Florian
management

entry.title: RAP Create button still enabled after disabling the feature
entry.content:
Hi !

I have created global feature with intention to disable CREATE action - it all works fine but instead of hiding cerate button it is still enabled and when i click it i get popup saying that create action is disabled - any advice on how to disable/hide the create button ?

SAPUI5 Version is 1.96.1
Here is my implementation of get global features:

 

 METHOD get_global_features.
    DATA lv_allowed_create TYPE boole_d.

    lv_allowed_create = zcl_qm_claim=>get_global_create_allowed( ).
    IF lv_allowed_create = abap_false.

      result-%create =  if_abap_behv=>fc-o-disabled.
    ENDIF.
    result-%update = if_abap_behv=>fc-o-enabled.
  ENDMETHOD.

entry.title: QM- Results recording
entry.content:
Dear Experts, 

I have a requirement from our internal customer and gain better understanding on how the data entry requirements are driven my the MIC Creation in the QM Module. Our production team has asked engineering to aid them in reducing the burden of entering MIC data during inspection of a device we manufacture inhouse. For this model line we have a shop order QTY of 1920 EA with a total of 12 MICs required over 23,000 check boxes of Pass or Fail to be entered per shop order, which run multiple times per week. The team is looking into what flexibility there may be in the QM system architecture that may allow them to simply ender ‘QTY Accepted’ and ‘QTY Rejected’ specifically for attributive (P/F) Functional Inspections, rather than using the auto generated 1,920 Pass or Fail boxes that populate when entering the MIC #. 

Any ideas are welcome. Simply put, I like to know is there any simpler way to minimize results recording for 1920 characters with ex. 1000 QTY Pass , 920 QTY Fails.

entry.title: Blog Series- 1: SAP C4C Service cloud V2 integration with S4 Hana Utility (ISU)
entry.content:
Hello everyone, I am starting a new blog series for service v2 integration with S4 Hana Utility and in the very first part I am going to explain some of the key features of Service V2, pros and cons - V1 and V2 and Service v2 utility key functionality, Utility Iflows and synchronous webservice name

I am sure everyone is aware of the Buzz word “V2” and some of the Key features of V2, Listing down few of them here-

Key Features of V2 Service Cloud

  • Uninterrupted solution availability by leveraging modern technology and Hyperscaler experience.
  • API-first/microservices based services
  • Complement the core solution with tool sets to quickly deliver new composable apps
  • Almost Zero Downtime
  • Agent Desktop
  • Case management
  • Robust Global search
  • Native Email Management

[Will Publish another blog series for v2 features in detail]

Pros and Cons

V2 vs V1

Pro’s

Con’s

SAP Sales and Service Cloud V2

•  Microservices architecture – enables improved performance, scalability and flexibility.

•       Advanced business capabilities, including native intelligent sales and service solutions.

•       Latest version for Sales and Service – benefit from future innovations from SAP

•       Industry solution for utilities lacks some features available in V1. However, per SAP, it is expected to be at feature parity with V1 by the end of 2024.

SAP Sales and Service Cloud V1

•       Industry solution for utilities is mature relative to V2, and several large utility companies are already live on it.

•       Performance may be an issue while accessing information related to large customers/ accounts.

Disclaimer- This is entirely based on current V2 and V1 solution and solely my inputs. Both V2 and V1 is equally robust product.

Prerequisites for integration of Service cloud V2 with S4 Hana System

  • Software component IS-UT 808
  • OSS Note 3450264 needs to implement to get all new features of SAP Service Cloud Version 2 release.

SAP Service Cloud Version 2, Utilities Functionality 

Please refer the image below 

Service IFLOW Details

  Service Cloud V2 Integration with SAP S4 HANA Utilities consist of three different packages.

  1. SAP Service Cloud V2 for Utilities Master Data Integration with SAP S/4HANA
  2. SAP Service Cloud V2 for Utilities Premise Integration with SAP S/4HANA
  3. SAP Service Cloud V2 for Utilities Contract Account Integration with SAP S/4HANA

Master data Integration is same as V1 and there is no change in the Webservice while there is change in the contract account replication.

Webservice Name

Business partner- Same as V1

Contract account- CO_FKK_MDG_CONTRACT_ACCOUNT_BU

Premise - CO_UTILITIES_TECHNICAL_OBJEST

 Webservices for synchronous services are different from V1, and we need to use the V2 version for integrating with Service cloud v2. Please check SAP NOTE- 3253818 to see the complete list provided by SAP, which is having all the service details with the Integration flow Name, SAP Cloud Integration Source Path, Source System, Target System URL, S/4HANA Service Name 

Topic for Part 2- Some of the key difference with respect to Integration of V1 and V2 with S4 Hana system, Replication model, Details of the webservices Configuration and  etc*. Stay tuned to get further deep dive. Please provide your feedback and comments.

Thank you 

Chanchal Jha

entry.title: Explore cost-efficiently the latest SAP S/4HANA Build with Guided Beta SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02
entry.content:
What is Guided Beta Testing?

Guided Beta is a program that provides SAP customers with the opportunity to test the latest SAP S/4HANA build before its official release. During this testing phase, customers can explore the new features and capabilities of the latest version of S/4HANA in their own system landscape, using their own data.

The benefits of Guided Beta Testing are numerous.
Firstly, it enables businesses to explore new products and releases cost-efficiently before the official release.
Additionally, the program provides customers with the opportunity to test their individual processes with their data in their system landscape, reducing risk and saving costs and time.

Guided Beta Testing also allows organizations to expand their individual SAP knowledge and network. By participating in the program, businesses can gain a deeper understanding of the latest features and capabilities and connect with other SAP users to exchange ideas and best practices.

Guided Beta is:

  • A Proof of Concept with the latest non-released S/4HANA Build before Release-to-Customer.
  • Specific new feature(s) introduced with the latest release of S/4HANA will be tested. End to end Regression test scenarios cannot be supported due to the limited timeframe of the program.
  • With Direct Solution Management & Development Involvement and free of charge support from SAP.

Within the Beta phase, customers will get access to the prototype of the software components listed below, as well as the complementary documentation:

  • SAP S/4HANA PRIVATE CLOUD 2023 FPS02 (Only relevant for SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Customers)
  • SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02 (Only relevant for SAP S/4HANA On-Premise Customers)

Other common software components for both categories of customers include:

  • SAP S/4HANA FOUNDATION 2023 FPS02
  • ABAP PLATFORM 2023 SPS02
  • SAP FIORI FOR SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPSS02
  • SAP FIORI FES 2023 for S/4HANA SPS02
  • SL TOOLSET 1.0 SPS41
          - SUM 2.0 SP20
          - SWPM 2.0 SP18

What is required from the customer?

To participate in the Guided Beta program, customers must commit to the rules and regulations of the program by signing a Test and Evaluation Agreement (TEA). They must also involve their resources in testing and provide a test system landscape, including remote access. Customers must test their processes and provide valid feedback, as the program aims to improve the platform before its official release.

Guided Beta Testing is an excellent opportunity to stay ahead of the competition by exploring the latest SAP S/4HANA build before its official release. This program provides customers with the ability to test their individual processes and provide feedback, which can help SAP to improve and provide better services to its customers.

More details can be found in this presentation.

If you’re interested in joining the program, fill out the questionnaire and  apply before July 26th, 2024 at: Beta_S4HANA@sap.com

entry.title: Mapping License with roles
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

We currently try to optimize the assigned named user license types for standard and custom roles and other authorization objects.

My current understanding already gives me the possibility to show me TCodes and the ACTVT corresponding to a certain role (AGR_1251 table), however when I now try to assign the most efficient license type (one that for example covers only the transactions granted to the role) either on the role or the used transactions, how could I get the information which license type covers which TCode / role from the SAP system?

As in the role-based user classification, I am able to see if a license covers a certain user with certain role, I would like to know if there is a way to understand which transactions (TCodes) or roles are covered by which license type.

In the rule-based user classification, one can define rules that provide you with a license type suitable for your rule set, but how is this matching established?

I would be grateful for any hints, no matter how far-fetched they are, whether it is a link between license types and standard roles, license types and transactions or even a link I am not aware of.

Thanks in advance and kind regards,

Johnny

Recently, I realized how important ERP systems are, and I’m really interested in becoming an ERP consultant. I have some experience using SAP in my current job, and it has sparked my interest in learning more about it.Switching careers mid-life can be challenging, but I am very motivated. My goal is to move from being an accountant to an ERP consultant, and I believe that gaining SAP certification will help me achieve this. This opportunity to learn and get certified in SAP is something I don’t want to miss this opportunity.

Thank you for providing us this opportunity.

Best regard,

Anh Duc Le

entry.title: Creating a personal development environment #1 - Why Neovim?
entry.content:
In this blog post series I want to share my journey on how I created my personal development environment for developing cloud native SAP applications. The focus here lies in the term "journey", as I am nowhere near a finished state and still learning a lot along the way. This is not a tutorial about Neovim (others can do that better) - this is more of a documentation of my journey with a bit of focus on SAP specific things.

As most developers these days, I was using VS Code for a long time and was not really thinking about my development environment much. VS Code is a fantastic IDE (integrated development environment) and most of the features one could ask for available out of the box, such as syntax highlighting, language servers, an integrated terminal, and a wide range of available extensions. It was not until some of my programming heroes kept talking about neovim over and over again and how great it was, until I became curious. Neovim is a Vim-based text editor, and it was just amazing to see how fast and efficient these people where with it, mostly because of not using the mouse at all and the great customization options. TJ DeVries established the term "personal development environment", and I realized I hadn't personalized my previous IDE (VS Code) at all and had almost no clue about the available keyboard shortcuts. After all, the mouse was always right there so I was not forced to learn any keyboard shortcuts. So I realized to become fast and efficient I had to go down the Neovim rabbit hole - and here we are.

At first, I was very underwhelmed with the experience. After all, Neovim keybindings are very unique and unusual (from a modern perspective) and almost every feature has to be configured explicitly. A raw Neovim installation therefore is exactly that - very raw. But the plugin ecosystem for Neovim is fantastic and there are plenty of resources available to learn how to configure Neovim (see the end of this post). So I started doing my homework, hacking away, and configuring my dotfiles.

As you can see in the screen shot above, Neovim comes with a file explorer out of the box, but it doesn't look like a file explorer you know from other environments where it would show on the left of the screen and always be visible. I knew I wanted to recreate what I was used to and needed a plugin for that. The plugin ecosystem of Neovim is great and it is fairly easy to install them - not VS Code easy, but fairly easy.

You need a packager manager to install plugins/packages (I'll use these terms interchangeably) and a popular choice is lazy.nvim. In my central Neovim configuration directory (~/.config/neovim/), I created an init.lua file with following code to install lazy.nvim:

local lazypath = vim.fn.stdpath("data") .. "/lazy/lazy.nvim"

if not vim.loop.fs_stat(lazypath) then
  vim.fn.system({
    "git",
    "clone",
    "--filter=blob:none",
    "https://github.com/folke/lazy.nvim.git",
    "--branch=stable", -- latest stable release
    lazypath,
  })
end

vim.opt.rtp:prepend(lazypath)

-- this looks for all lua files in the lua/plugins/ directory
require("lazy").setup("plugins")

Ok, lazy.nvim is now installed and as you can see in the code, it apparently looks for a plugins/ directory to find all plugins. But let's take a step back and appreciate the fact that we are to write this code in Lua, which is a powerful, efficient, lightweight, embeddable scripting language. Its syntax is similar to JavaScript and it's very easy to use.

I was now ready to install my first plugin. I created a new file at ~/.config/nvim/lua/plugins/filetree.lua (the file name itself doesn't really matter) with the following content:

vim.g.loaded_netrw = 1
vim.g.loaded_netrwPlugin = 1

return {
    {
        "nvim-tree/nvim-tree.lua",
        config = function()
            require("nvim-tree").setup({
                -- call empty function to not apply default and interfere with telescope
                on_attach = function() end,
                git = {
                    ignore = false
                }
            })

            local api = require "nvim-tree.api"

            -- reimplement some of the default keybindings only for NvimTree files
            vim.api.nvim_create_autocmd("FileType", {
                pattern = { "NvimTree" },
                callback = function()
                    vim.keymap.set("n", "<Enter>", api.node.open.edit, { buffer = true })
                    vim.keymap.set("n", "fv", api.node.open.vertical, { buffer = true })
                    vim.keymap.set("n", "r", api.fs.rename, { buffer = true })
                    vim.keymap.set("n", "d", api.fs.remove, { buffer = true })
                    vim.keymap.set("n", "n", api.fs.create, { buffer = true })
                end
            })

            vim.keymap.set("n", "fe", ":NvimTreeFocus<Enter>")
        end
    },
    {
        "nvim-tree/nvim-web-devicons"
    }
}

This code disables the default netrw file explorer (lines 1 and 2) and then returns a lua table that lazy.nvim is on the lookout for. The nvim-tree plugin as well as the corresponding icons (nvim-web-devicons) get installed and while being at it, I also set up a few keybindings I want to use with nvim-tree. This is where the personalization and the quest for a "personal development environment" really starts. I decided to map rename, remove, and create to the first letters of the terms I associate them with, so r -> rename, d -> delete (remove), and n -> new (create).
I also decided to map more global operations that have to do with the file explorer to keybindings that start with the letter f (for file in my head). I use this same approach for other things like the language server and comments too, but we will get to that. This principle has really helped me learn and memorize the new keybindings fast. So to navigate to the file explorer is use fe -> file explorer and to open a file in a new vertical window I use fv -> file vertical.

This is what my Neovim looked like with nvim-tree file explorer opened. Still pretty rough, but it's a good starting point to continue personalizing it. We will continue with some miscellaneous configuration (for example a nice color scheme) in the next post.

Resources

  • typecraft on YouTube
  • DevOnDuty on YouTube

entry.title: Re: CloudEvents at SAP 🌁
entry.content: @Lidia-Sarasua, a public EDA strategy document is in the works, and that should help you highlight the differences and help decide which one to select.

entry.title: Re: CloudEvents at SAP 🌁
entry.content:
@BrianChanthat's a very good question.... deciding when to use data or notification events. As you correctly highlighted, there are performance and security considerations, and it might just end up depending on your needs. Security is a critical aspect, and in some platforms, it might be possible to establish ACLs, but then we add another layer of complexity there. I guess there is no right or wrong answer; sometimes a notification event might be enough. In other scenarios, the payload data might not be sensitive, and a data event will be great. Then there are times when you don't know when the event will be consumed and if it is not consumed in real-time the data might become stale/irrelevant if processed later.

There are many factors to take into consideration, and there might not be a straightforward answer to this dilemma.

The error "HTTP_MAX_CONN_EXCEEDED" in your GSTR1 RAP application in SAP S4 HANA Public Cloud is caused by the maximum number of HTTP client connections being exceeded. This occurs when multiple HTTP requests are generated for an instance and cannot be handled. To resolve this issue, you can try the following:

Reduce the Number of HTTP Requests

Check if there are any unnecessary HTTP requests being generated in your application. Try to minimize the number of requests by optimizing your code or using more efficient methods.


Increase the Maximum Number of Connections:
You can increase the maximum number of HTTP client connections by configuring the max_connections parameter in your SAP system. However, be cautious when doing so, as it may impact system performance.

entry.title: Business Catalog for SSCUI Activity
entry.content:
Hello,

I just would like to know which business catalog is necessary for Define Plant App in Business Process Configuration in SAP CBC. Where can I look up the required business catalog for missing authorization?

Thanks.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Business Catalog for SSCUI Activity
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

 

In the Project Experience, after searching for the Configuration activity you can see the ID number for this activity.

Having the ID number, you can find what catalog is needed for each configuration after downloading the Excel file Configuration apps from the Best Practices Explorer accelerators:

https://rapid.sap.com/bp/BP_CLD_ENTPR
*Please login to the Best Practices Explorer for being able to see all the accelerators.
 
After you search on IMG_Act BusinessCatalog Match sheet with the numeric ID of the configuration activity the business catalog will be displayed.
 
I have checked and the Business Catalog related to Define Plant activity is SAP_CA_BC_IC_LND_MM_IM0_PC.
 
You can check the SAP Note 3238660 - CBC- How to find the business catalog to access a SSCUI (Configuration Activity) and avoid authorization errors for screenshots and step by step in case you still have any doubts regarding the process to find the Business Catalog.
 
Kind regards.

entry.title: Re: Can you provide suggestions for migrating data from S...
entry.content:
Hello @KaviM,

The data migration in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition can only be achieved through the Migration Cockpit, hence no iFlows or custom programs can be used.

Please note that you can use an ETL tool (SAP or third-party) to fill the staging tables for both local and remote database approaches. However, if you would like to populate the data directly on database layer, you can use a remote SAP HANA database hosted on SAP BTP. 

For more detailed information: Migrate your Data to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Hope this helps.

Arturo Montes de Oca

entry.title: Re: Unable to suppress group footer section based on cond...
entry.content:
It sounds like, currently, you have all 4 subreports placed in Group Footer 1 (GF1).
Right-click GF1 and select 'Insert Section Below'. That would result in GF1a and GF1b sections. Repeat twice more to get GF1c and GF1d as well.

Place 1st subreport in GF1a, 2nd subreport in GF1b, etc.

Use the dynamic 'Suppress' expression for each of these sections to Suppress the section when the current group value (what you call 'Header') does not match the one for that subreport.

For example, in the Suppress expression for GF1a, have something like:
---------------------------
{Header} <> "Header 1"
---------------------------

What is a Sap?

SAP, an acronym for Systems, Applications, and Products in Data Processing, is a cornerstone in the world of enterprise resource planning (ERP) software. Developed by the German company SAP SE, this suite of integrated applications is designed to streamline and centralize various business operations, fostering efficiency and enhancing productivity across organizations of all sizes.

The Genesis of SAP
Founded in 1972 by five former IBM engineers, SAP SE set out to develop software that would provide real-time data processing capabilities. The initial goal was to create a system that could integrate all business processes and share data across different departments within an organization. This vision materialized into SAP R/2 and later SAP R/3, which revolutionized the ERP landscape.

The Core of SAP: ERP Software
At its heart, SAP is an ERP system that integrates various business processes into a single, unified system. This integration ensures that data flows seamlessly between different departments, reducing redundancy, improving accuracy, and enabling real-time decision-making. The SAP ERP system covers a broad spectrum of business functions, including finance, sales, inventory management, production planning, and human resources.

Key SAP Modules
SAP's modular architecture is one of its defining features. Each module is tailored to specific business needs and functions, allowing organizations to implement only the modules relevant to their operations. Here are some of the core SAP modules:

FI (Financial Accounting): This module manages financial transactions, general ledger, accounts receivable and payable, and financial reporting. It ensures compliance with accounting standards and regulatory requirements.

CO (Controlling): Focused on internal reporting, this module helps businesses track and manage costs and operational performance. It supports budgeting, variance analysis, and profitability analysis.

SD (Sales and Distribution): SD handles order processing, pricing, shipping, billing, and sales data. It streamlines the sales cycle, from order creation to product delivery and invoicing.

MM (Materials Management): This module oversees procurement, inventory management, and material requirement planning. It helps in optimizing supply chain operations and maintaining adequate inventory levels.

PP (Production Planning): PP manages production schedules, manufacturing processes, and resource allocation. It ensures efficient production operations and timely fulfillment of manufacturing orders.

HR (Human Resources): The HR module covers employee management, payroll, recruitment, training, and performance appraisal. It aids in maintaining a well-managed and motivated workforce.

Real-Time Data Processing
One of SAP's significant advantages is its ability to process data in real time. This capability allows businesses to respond promptly to market changes, customer demands, and operational challenges. Real-time data processing enhances decision-making, improves accuracy, and enables proactive management.

entry.title: JSON model loading after the view is rendered, thus leaving the view blank
entry.content: I am facing a problem in sapui5 where my view is loading before the model maintained in the manifest.json is pulling data from the given json file and because of this the view is rendering blank. I have to refresh the page manually for view to wait for model to load before the view is rendered. I have used attachRequestCompleted function on the model to get model data but sometimes while navigating from one view to another I am facing this issue. I have also maintained preload: true for model in manifest. I can see the json file loading in the network tab. How can I solve this issue, does anyone have any idea?

The error "HTTP_MAX_CONN_EXCEEDED" in your GSTR1 RAP application in SAP S4 HANA Public Cloud is caused by the maximum number of HTTP client connections being exceeded. This occurs when multiple HTTP requests are generated for an instance and cannot be handled. To resolve this issue, you can try the following:

Reduce the Number of HTTP Requests:
Check if there are any unnecessary HTTP requests being generated in your application. Try to minimize the number of requests by optimizing your code or using more efficient methods.
Increase the Maximum Number of Connections:
You can increase the maximum number of HTTP client connections by configuring the max_connections parameter in your SAP system. However, be cautious when doing so, as it may impact system performance.
Use a More Efficient HTTP Client:
Consider using a more efficient HTTP client library that can handle a larger number of connections without causing the error.
